description.task: Write a Java program that demonstrates arithmetic, relational, and logical operators using two integer variables x = 15 and y = 4.
instructions: Use x = 15 and y = 4 as provided.
Print the sum (x + y), difference (x - y), product (x * y), quotient (x / y), and remainder (x % y) each prefixed with a label.
Print the boolean result of the relational expressions: x > y and x == y.
Print the boolean result of the logical expressions: (x > 10 && y > 2) and (x < 5 || y > 2).
description.hint: Integer division in Java truncates the decimal part — 15 / 4 evaluates to 3, not 3.75.
description.expectedOutput:
Sum: 19
Difference: 11
Product: 60
Quotient: 3
Remainder: 3
x > y: true
x == y: false
x > 10 && y > 2: true
x < 5 || y > 2: true

description.task: Write a Java program that calculates the Body Mass Index (BMI) given a weight of 70 kg and a height of 1.75 m, and prints the result.
description.context: BMI is a real-world use of arithmetic operators. It is calculated as weight divided by the square of height.
instructions: Use the given weight (70.0) and height (1.75) values.
Apply the formula: bmi = weight / (height * height).
Print all three lines in the exact format shown.
Do NOT round the result — print the full double value.

description.task: Write a Java program that demonstrates the difference between pre-increment (++x) and post-increment (x++) operators.
description.context: The position of ++ matters: pre-increment increments first then returns the new value; post-increment returns the current value and then increments.
instructions: Run the starter code as-is — do not change it.
Observe and verify the four output lines.
Add a comment block after the code explaining the difference in your own words.
Submit the code including your explanation comment.
description.hint: int postResult = a++ means: first assign a (5) to postResult, then increment a to 6. int preResult = ++b means: first increment b to 6, then assign 6 to preResult.
description.expectedOutput:
Post-increment result: 5
a after a++: 6
Pre-increment result: 6
b after ++b: 6
